bundle_parser.identified_role: parses the login-result-0x3F continuation payload and hands the decoded strings into the desktop-auth continuation branch
key_observations: Consumes five NUL-terminated strings from the decoded continuation buffer after the normal packet-cipher/buffer helper at 0x14016BBF0 has run on connection +0x2B8.
Passes the fifth parsed string to 0x1401303A0 first, then forwards the first four parsed strings to 0x14029BAB0.
The post-handoff state write at 0x1401599A4 sets client +0x10 to 0x5A, showing that the quartet handoff is a concrete state-machine transition rather than an inert parse.

separate_fifth_string_consumer.notes: The fifth parsed continuation string is still routed separately and remains semantically unresolved on this pass.

continuation_token_exchange_builder.identified_role: builds the first concrete shield/oauth/token request from the login-result-0x3F continuation quartet
request_object_lifecycle: Allocates a fresh 0x268-byte request-like object and replaces the previous global object at 0x140E58A78.
Attaches HTTP-style Authorization, Content-Type, and Accept headers to that request object.

authorization_header.notes: Reuses the earlier desktop-auth config cache to build a Basic-auth header before the continuation request is submitted.

endpoint.notes: This is the same cached OAuth root used by the later shield/oauth/check_token and shield/oauth/token refresh builders.

state_effects: Calls 0x140299960 with the assembled endpoint and request payload.
Writes 0x140E58A70 = 2 at 0x14029BCBB, marking the request as the token-exchange stage in the shared auth request state machine.

adjacent_bearer_play_request.identified_role: builds a Bearer-authenticated public game-session launch request using the cached JX access token
key_observations: Formats an Authorization header from the literal 'Bearer' at 0x140B5DD58 and the cached JX access token at 0x140E58AA0.
Reuses the same request object/header pattern as the continuation token exchange: Authorization, Content-Type, and Accept are added before dispatch.
Composes the endpoint from the OAuth-root slot 0x140E58B68 plus the literal path 'public/v1/games/YCfdbvr2pM1zUYMxJRexZY/play' at 0x140B698A0.
Calls the same request-dispatch helper family and writes 0x140E58A70 = 5 while also clearing 0x140E58B98.
adjacent_bearer_play_request.notes: This builder sits immediately earlier in the same auth-oriented function cluster and is a concrete downstream consumer of the cached JX access token, but this pass did not fully prove its exact ordering relative to the quartet-fed shield/oauth/token exchange.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.desktop_auth_config_cache: This pass closes the earlier gap between the parsed continuation quartet and the cached desktop-auth config bundle: 0x14029BAB0 is now proven to be a concrete shield/oauth/token request builder that consumes both sources at once.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.jx_cache_dispatch: The adjacent Bearer-auth play request ties the later cached JX_ACCESS_TOKEN at 0x140E58AA0 back into the same broader post-0x3F auth/session pipeline.

updated_conclusion: The login-result-0x3F continuation is now mapped as a concrete request handoff: 0x140159810 parses the quartet grant_type/token/redirTo/sig, 0x14029BAB0 immediately turns it into a Basic-authenticated shield/oauth/token request using the cached desktop-auth config block, and a nearby builder at 0x14029B1D0 consumes the later JX access token to issue a Bearer-authenticated public game launch request on the same service root.
next_targets: Resolve the exact semantic meaning of the fifth continuation string still routed separately to 0x1401303A0.
Prove the exact request ordering between the Bearer-auth public/v1/games/.../play stage and the quartet-fed shield/oauth/token exchange.
Trace the upstream producers of client +0x19D88 and client +0x19E30 so the cached OAuth and game-session base roots can be tied to concrete bootstrap/config sources.
